sql_findings.query_summary: Analysis of shared device fingerprints in withdrawals.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified multiple instances where the same device fingerprint was used by different customers (e.g., FRA and RUS accounts sharing a fingerprint), suggesting a shared infrastructure for fraudulent activity.

sql_findings.query_summary: Investigation of recipient accounts and risk scores.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found a specific recipient account (MX012345678901234567890123456) receiving 5 withdrawals from 3 different device fingerprints, all of which were flagged with high-risk scores (0.63-0.67) and indicators like 'velocity' and 'card_error_history'.

web_references.title: Visitor Fingerprint: Device Fingerprinting for Fraud and ... - Sardine
web_references.relevance: Describes how device fingerprints and velocity are used to detect fraud rings.

web_references.title: Device Intelligence - Unit21
web_references.relevance: Explains the use of high-velocity fingerprints in credential stuffing and fraud rings.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify shared infrastructure (IP/Device) across different customer accounts with high risk scores.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that IP 41.44.55.66 and device fingerprint 'deadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeef' were used to access at least two different customer accounts (80320da3 and 47f31044) for 6 high-risk withdrawals. Another shared fingerprint 'a1b2c3d4...' was also linked to multiple accounts.

sql_findings.query_summary: Evaluate the specific risk indicators triggered for the flagged withdrawals.
sql_findings.result_summary: Withdrawals consistently triggered 'geographic_risk' (scores up to 0.90), 'velocity' (scores up to 0.80), and 'device_fingerprint' (scores up to 0.86) indicators, confirming the 'impossible travel' and 'unauthorized device' narrative.

web_references.title: How to Detect Early Signs of Customer Account Takeovers
web_references.relevance: Provides a direct definition and detection strategy for 'impossible travel' and 'new device' logins as primary indicators of ATO.

web_references.title: Account Takeover Fraud - Industry Use Cases
web_references.relevance: Explains the 'Credential Stuffing' and 'Impossible Travel' patterns in a banking context, matching the observed data.

sql_findings.query_summary: Investigation of withdrawal decisions for 'minimal trading' or 'deposit-and-run' indicators.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified multiple escalated withdrawals from customers sharing IP addresses (e.g., 41.44.55.66) and device fingerprints (e.g., 'deadbeef...').

sql_findings.query_summary: Analysis of trading volume and trade counts for flagged customers.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that 75% of the suspected accounts had 2 or fewer trades with total volumes under $20, despite requesting withdrawals of up to $2,990.

sql_findings.query_summary: Cross-referencing IPs and devices across the customer base.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found specific IP-Device pairs (e.g., IP 185.199.108.55 with rotating fingerprints) that indicate automated or professional fraud operations.

web_references.title: FCA: Money Laundering through Markets Review
web_references.relevance: Directly describes 'abnormal funding patterns' including 'no trading between deposits and withdrawals' as a key money laundering risk.

web_references.title: CBUAE AML Typologies in the Financial Sector
web_references.relevance: Defines 'minimal transactional activity' and 'matching turnovers' as indicators of professional money laundering services.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify accounts sharing device fingerprints and their geographic registration.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found two clusters: one sharing a device between Russia and France, and another sharing a device within Egypt. Both clusters had high-risk (blocked/escalated) withdrawal decisions.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyze trading volume and PnL for accounts in the shared-device clusters.
sql_findings.result_summary: Accounts showed minimal trading (0-2 trades) with negligible PnL (e.g., $0.02, $0.50), indicating trading is a facade for fund movement.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check for shared withdrawal recipient accounts and card errors.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that two accounts in Egypt shared the exact same IBAN for withdrawals. One account in the FRA/RUS cluster had 3 distinct card transaction errors.

web_references.title: Fraud Types and Authentication for Remote Payment Use Cases
web_references.relevance: Describes the use of velocity checks and device fingerprints to identify card testing and rapid fund flows.

web_references.title: Statistical Fraud Detection: A Review
web_references.relevance: Discusses the detection of 'large deposit followed immediately by a similar sized withdrawal' as a key indicator of money laundering.

title: Credit Card Cashout (Carding) / Pass-through Money Laundering
formal_pattern_name: Credit Card Cashout (Carding) / Pass-through Money Laundering

sql_findings.query_summary: Analysis of withdrawal-to-deposit ratios and trade counts for flagged accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified multiple accounts withdrawing >95% of deposits with <2 total trades. For example, one user withdrew $2990 of a $3000 deposit with only 1 trade.

sql_findings.query_summary: Infrastructure overlap check (IP and Device Fingerprint).
sql_findings.result_summary: Found two accounts sharing IP 41.44.55.66 and a suspicious device fingerprint 'deadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeef'.

sql_findings.query_summary: Transaction history and error code analysis.
sql_findings.result_summary: Detected 'card_restricted' errors on failed deposit attempts for a flagged customer, confirming card testing/carding behavior.

web_references.title: Online investment scams: Inside a fake trading platform - Netcraft
web_references.relevance: Describes the use of fake trading platforms for money laundering and the importance of monitoring withdrawal-to-deposit ratios.

web_references.title: 10 SIGNS OF A SCAM CRYPTO OR FOREX TRADING WEBSITE (CFTC)
web_references.relevance: Outlines signs of fraudulent trading activity, including inconsistent financial behavior and lack of economic purpose.

sql_findings.query_summary: Shared device and recipient account lookup
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed ahmed.hassan@example.com and fatima.nour@example.com share device 'deadbeef...' and bank account 'EG800002000156789012345678901'.

sql_findings.query_summary: Recipient name overlap analysis
sql_findings.result_summary: Found massive overlap in recipient names: 'Elena Volkov' is a recipient for 9 unique customers, and 'John Doe' for 8 customers, indicating a large-scale mule network.

web_references.title: Graph-Based Approaches for Detecting Fraud Rings
web_references.relevance: Describes the structure of coordinated fraud rings using shared resources.

web_references.title: Device Intelligence: The Hidden Layer of Fraud Prevention
web_references.relevance: Explains the use of device fingerprinting to link mule accounts.

sql_findings.query_summary: Search for shared device fingerprints and recipient accounts among flagged withdrawals.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified a cluster of two accounts (47f31044 and 80320da3) sharing device 'deadbeef...' and recipient account 'EG800002...'.

sql_findings.query_summary: Detailed timeline and attribute analysis for the linked accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed both accounts registered simultaneously (2026-02-01 12:00:00) and performed synchronized withdrawals on 2026-02-08 12:00:00 from IP 41.44.55.66.

web_references.title: Visitor Fingerprint: Device Fingerprinting for Fraud - Sardine
web_references.relevance: Describes how shared device fingerprints and IP addresses are used to link coordinated fraud rings.

web_references.title: Application Value Of Device Fingerprinting In Payment Fraud Prevention
web_references.relevance: Explains the value of device fingerprinting in identifying bulk account registration and coordinated payment fraud.

title: Carding and Collusive Money Laundering Ring
formal_pattern_name: Carding and Collusive Money Laundering Ring

sql_findings.query_summary: Shared Device Fingerprint Analysis
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified two distinct device fingerprints ('deadbeef...', 'a1b2c3d4...') each shared by multiple new accounts with high-risk profiles.

sql_findings.query_summary: Withdrawal Recipient Account Linkage
sql_findings.result_summary: Found a single bank account (IBAN ending in '5678901') being used as the destination for withdrawals from multiple different customer accounts, confirming collusion.

sql_findings.query_summary: Transaction Error Deep-Dive
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed multiple 'card_restricted' error codes on the flagged accounts, a hallmark of stolen card testing (carding).

web_references.title: Types of Credit Card Fraud and Prevention Strategies - FOCAL
web_references.relevance: Describes the 'carding' process and the use of rapid transactions/shared devices.
